 var transferSummaries = await _dbContext.MaterialTransactionTrackings
            .Where(ts => ts.MaterialTransferSummary == id)

                SubLocation = mts.RouteDetailNavigation.RouteLocationNavigation.LocationName,
                MainLocation = mts.RouteDetailNavigation.RouteLocationNavigation.MainLocation.LocationName,
                Approver = mts.Approver,
                Status = string.IsNullOrEmpty(mts.Approver) ? "On Progression" : "Approved",
                ApproverList = string.Join(", ", _dbContext.AuthorityLocations

The extra approver may be caused by the incorrect usage of the ng-container in your HTML code. Make sure that you are only displaying the "Approver Authority" if the "AuthorityApprove" property is true in the transferSummaries object. Here's the updated code to achieve that: ```html
Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}}
``` This will ensure that the "Approver Authority" is only displayed when the "AuthorityApprove" property is true.

Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}}

Apologies for the confusion. The issue seems to be with the ng-container condition. Here's the corrected code: ```html
Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}}
``` In this code, I've introduced an else statement with a template reference variable (`#dateApproved`) to handle the scenario when `route.approver` is not null. This will ensure that the "Date Approved" section is only displayed when `route.modifyDate` is not null.

Apologies for the confusion once again. It seems that the issue lies in the conditions of the ng-container blocks. Here's the updated code: ```html
Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
 Approver Authority : {{route.approverList}}
``` In this code, I've used ng-container within ng-template blocks to separate the logic based on the conditions you mentioned. The "Approver" and "Date Approved" sections are displayed if route.approver is true and route.modifyDate is not null. The "Approver Authority" section is displayed if route.approver is false and route.authorityApprove is true.

Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}}

I apologize for the confusion. To remove the duplicated "Approver" section, you can update the code as follows: ```html
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}} Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
``` In this code, I introduced two ng-templates: `showApproverAuthority` and `showApproverDate`. 1. If both `route.approver` and `route.modifyDate` are present, we will skip the `showApproverAuthority` ng-template. 2. If either `route.approver` or `route.modifyDate` is missing, we will show the `showApproverAuthority` ng-template, which displays the "Approver Authority" section only if `route.authorityApprove` is true. 3. If both `route.approver` and `route.modifyDate` are present, we will show the `showApproverDate` ng-template, which displays the "Approver" and "Date Approved" sections.

there are no skip, the condition is only with route.approver, if the data exist show approver: and date approver: else Approver authority: shown
Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}}

Apologies for the confusion. If you only want to show the "Approver" and "Date Approved" sections if `route.approver` exists, and show the "Approver Authority" section if `route.approver` does not exist and `route.authorityApprove` is true, you can update the code as follows: ```html
Approver authority : {{route.approverList}} Approver : {{route.approver}}
Date Approved : {{route.modifyDate}}
``` In this code, if `route.approver` exists, it will enter the first ng-container and display nothing because it is empty. If `route.approver` does not exist, it will enter the else block and display the "Approver Authority" section only if `route.authorityApprove` is true. Then, outside both the ng-container and ng-template blocks, there is another ng-container that checks if both `route.approver` and `route.modifyDate` exist, and if so, displays the "Approver" and "Date Approved" sections.
